name: Hina
description: Hina is a powerful and multifaceted goddess in Hawaiian mythology, often associated with the moon, female productivity, kapa making, and healing. She is a primordial deity, appearing in various forms and stories throughout the islands. Some accounts depict her as the wife of the god Kū, while others portray her as a strong, independent woman who leaves her husband to seek a better life. Hina's influence extends to the natural world, influencing tides, weaving, and the growth of plants. She is revered as a creative force and a symbol of female empowerment. One prominent myth describes her journey to the moon to escape the heat of the sun, where she eventually learned to control its power, bringing balance to the world. Her connection to the moon establishes her as a lunar deity, influencing cycles and rhythms. Hina is also a skilled craftswoman, particularly known for her expertise in kapa making, the art of creating cloth from bark. She taught these skills to women, empowering them and contributing to the cultural heritage of Hawaii. Hina's stories are rich with symbolism, representing resilience, resourcefulness, and the importance of balance in the world. She embodies the feminine principle and is a vital figure in Hawaiian spiritual beliefs. Her legends continue to inspire and inform Hawaiian cultural practices, emphasizing the importance of creativity, healing, and respect for the natural world. She is revered as a protector and guide, embodying the strength and wisdom of the feminine divine.
subtitle: Goddess of the Moon and Kapa Making

keyMyths.title: Hina's Journey to the Moon
keyMyths.description: Hina, tired of the sun's heat, travels to the moon. There, she learns to control the moon's power and brings balance to the world.

keyMyths.title: Hina and Kapa Making
keyMyths.description: Hina is credited with inventing kapa making, the art of creating cloth from bark. She taught this skill to women, greatly contributing to Hawaiian culture.

keyMyths.title: Hina and Maui
keyMyths.description: In some stories, Hina is Maui's mother or grandmother. These stories often involve Maui's heroic deeds and Hina's guidance and support.

sources.author: Martha Warren Beckwith
sources.description: A comprehensive compilation of Hawaiian myths and legends.
sources.text: A comprehensive compilation of Hawaiian myths and legends.

sources.author: Rubellite Kawena Johnson
sources.work: Kumulipo: The Hawaiian Hymn of Creation
sources.description: An analysis of the Kumulipo, a Hawaiian creation chant, which mentions Hina.
